summary: mailconfirmer ships a postinstall Windows dropper that fetches and executes an XOR-encrypted binary
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (`install-hook.js`) executes an embedded UTF-16LE base64 PowerShell payload via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <payload>`. The decoded PowerShell downloads `launcher.bin` from `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin`, XOR-decrypts the bytes with key 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, runs it with `Start-Process... -WindowStyle Hidden`, then deletes the file. After launching the payload, the script performs anti-forensic cleanup: it removes the `mailconfirmer` entry from the host project's `package.json` and `package-lock.json`, kills a `dllhost.exe` watchdog under `%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Caches`, and `fs.rmSync`'s its own module directory, with a `MoveFileEx(..., 0x4)` reboot-on-delete fallback to scrub itself from disk. The payload is opaque, encrypted, and hosted from an unrelated personal GitHub account — it has no connection to the package's advertised email-confirmation purpose; `index.js` only contains stub functions that `console.log`. Any Windows developer or build agent that runs `npm install` for this package is compromised by an attacker-controlled binary, and the malicious dependency is then hidden from the project's manifest files.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
